features.name: Mol* (Molstar) 3D Structure Viewer
features.description: Standalone 3D viewer backed by the Mol*/RCSB Molstar engine for large biological molecules with support for cartoon, ball-and-stick, and other molecular representations.
features.evidence_summary: Viewer registration (role: viewer,panel), programmatic open functions (viewBiostructure/viewPdbById/viewPdbByData), context-menu item, and the dedicated help page all share the Biostructure/Molstar name root.

features.name: NGL 3D Structure Viewer
features.description: Standalone 3D viewer backed by the NGL library for large biological molecules, supporting formats such as mmtf, cns, gro, prmtop, and others.
features.evidence_summary: Viewer function (role: viewer,panel), NGL GL service accessor, context-menu item, and dedicated help page all share the NGL name root.

features.name: Biotrack Sequence Track Viewer
features.description: Viewer displaying polymer annotation tracks (secondary structure, sequence features, etc.) for biological sequences using the Saguaro engine.
features.evidence_summary: Viewer registration (role: viewer,panel) and test apps share the Biotrack/saguaro name root; the combined app shows Molstar + Biotrack used together.

features.name: In-Grid Structure Cell Renderer
features.description: Renders 3D protein and small-molecule structure thumbnails directly inside Datagrok grid cells for PDB-ID and chemical structure columns, using both Molstar and NGL backends.
features.evidence_summary: Both cell renderers (role: cellRenderer) and all in-grid demo/test apps target the same pattern: 3D structure thumbnails rendered inside grid cells.

features.name: Structure File Import
features.description: File-handler registrations that allow Datagrok to open a wide variety of 3D structure formats (PDB, mmCIF, GRO, XYZ, mmtf, ply, obj, ccp4, and more) directly from the file browser.
features.evidence_summary: Import functions all carry role: fileHandler with ext metadata covering the same domain of structure file formats; readAsText helpers support file-content loading.

features.name: Structure File Preview
features.description: File-browser preview panels that render structure, surface, density, and topology biostructure files inline when a user inspects a file in Datagrok.
features.evidence_summary: All six functions carry role: fileViewer and share the preview+Ngl/Biostructure naming pattern, covering different sub-formats of the same file-preview use case.

features.name: RCSB PDB Structure Fetch
features.description: Fetches protein structures from the RCSB Protein Data Bank by PDB ID in PDB, mmCIF, or BinaryCIF format, and can bulk-retrieve amino-acid sequences for a whole table column.
features.evidence_summary: Three RCSB-prefixed data-provider functions share the same retrieval pattern (PDB ID → structure data); Fetch PDB Sequences extends this to table columns; getPdbHelper supports the same PDB-fetch workflow.

features.name: PDB Context Panel & 3D Structure Widget
features.description: Context-panel widgets that automatically display a 3D structure preview and PDB metadata when the user selects a row with a PDB-ID or biostructure semantic type, and a panel for opening the residues table.

members.id: func:BiostructureViewer:Open PDB residues table menu item

features.evidence_summary: Panel/widget functions (role: panel or widgets,panel) surface 3D structure information in the context panel; residue-table functions expose the same structural data in tabular form.

features.name: Docking Conformations Viewer
features.description: Imports PDBQT docking-result files containing multiple ligand poses and displays them alongside the target protein structure, enabling visual inspection of docking results.
features.evidence_summary: importPdbqt is the file entry point for docking data (description: 'docking result ligand poses'); demoBioDockingConformations and dockingDemo both explicitly describe displaying ligand poses along the structure.

features.name: BiostructureData Serialization API
features.description: Programmatic API for packing and unpacking BiostructureData values as JSON, with context-menu actions to copy or download the raw structure value from any viewer.
features.evidence_summary: biostructureDataToJson packs BiostructureData to JSON; Copy/Download raw value actions expose the same underlying data for external use; all share the BiostructureData-value concept.
